name: Frozen
description: Disney's Frozen films, Arendelle, Elsa & Anna, and the songs the world can't unhear.

questions.q: What is the name of the fictional kingdom in 'Frozen'?

source.notes: Inspired by Norway.

questions.q: Who are the two royal sisters at the heart of 'Frozen'?
What magical power does Elsa possess?
Which Hans Christian Andersen fairy tale loosely inspired 'Frozen'?

source.notes: Andersen's 1844 fairy tale.

questions.q: What is the name of Kristoff's loyal reindeer?
Which character is a magical talking snowman who loves summer?

source.notes: Voiced by Josh Gad.

questions.q: Who is revealed as the true villain in the original 'Frozen'?

source.notes: 13th Prince of the Southern Isles.

questions.q: What act of love thaws Anna's frozen heart at the climax?

source.notes: Anna jumps in front of Elsa to take Hans's sword strike — sisterly love is the act.

questions.q: Who voices Elsa in the 'Frozen' films?

source.notes: Demi Lovato performs the Disney Channel-style end-credits version of 'Let It Go.'

questions.q: Who voices Anna in the 'Frozen' films?
Who wrote the original songs for 'Frozen' (including 'Let It Go')?

source.notes: Husband-wife duo; Robert is one of the few EGOT winners.

questions.q: Which 'Frozen' song won the Academy Award for Best Original Song in 2014?
choices: Do You Want to Build a Snowman?
For the First Time in Forever

questions.q: What Academy Award did 'Frozen' (2013) win in addition to Best Original Song?
Who co-directed 'Frozen' with Chris Buck and became Disney Animation's first female director?

source.notes: Lee later became Disney Animation's CCO.

questions.q: What is Kristoff's profession?
What is the name of the giant snow monster Elsa creates to guard her ice palace?
In 'Frozen 2', what is the name of the salamander-like fire spirit Elsa befriends?
In 'Frozen 2', what magical horse-like water spirit does Elsa tame?

source.notes: Norwegian/Scottish folklore: the Nøkk.

questions.q: What is the name of the indigenous people from the Enchanted Forest in 'Frozen 2'?

source.notes: Inspired by the Sámi people of northern Scandinavia.

questions.q: Did 'Frozen' (2013) become the highest-grossing animated film of all time during its theatrical run?
choices: Yes — surpassing Toy Story 3 in 2014, held #1 until The Lion King (2019)

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: Toy Story 3 (2010) still held the record at Frozen's 2013 release; Frozen surpassed it in early 2014. Reworded to reflect 'during theatrical run.' Held the all-time animated record from 2014 until Lion King (2019).

questions.q: What is the name of Elsa's younger sister and the film's co-protagonist?

source.notes: Voiced by Kristen Bell.

questions.q: What is the name of Anna's childhood-favorite snowman, reanimated by Elsa's magic?

source.notes: Voiced by Josh Gad.

questions.q: What is the name of the rugged ice-harvester who helps Anna find Elsa?

source.notes: Voiced by Jonathan Groff.

questions.q: What is the name of Kristoff's best friend, a reindeer?

source.notes: Sven 'talks' through Kristoff doing the voice.

questions.q: Which Oscar-winning power ballad does Elsa sing after fleeing Arendelle?
choices: 'Do You Want to Build a Snowman?'

source.notes: Won Best Original Song at the 2014 Oscars.

questions.q: The kingdom of Arendelle is inspired by which country's landscape?

source.notes: Bergen was a key reference for art direction.

questions.q: Who wrote the songs for Frozen, including 'Let It Go'?

source.notes: The married couple also wrote songs for Coco and WandaVision.

questions.q: What is the name of the southern island kingdom Hans is from?

source.notes: Hans is the youngest of 13 brothers.

questions.q: Which duchy's visiting Duke makes Elsa extremely wary, voiced by Alan Tudyk?

source.notes: Anna and Elsa repeatedly call him 'the Duke of Weaseltown.'

questions.q: What act of love breaks Elsa's curse on Anna?

source.notes: Subverts the traditional 'true love's kiss' Disney formula.

questions.q: What is Olaf's biggest dream in Frozen, humorously at odds with his physical form?

source.notes: 'In Summer' is Olaf's signature song.

questions.q: What year was the original Frozen released?

source.notes: Released November 27, 2013.

questions.q: Frozen II was released in what year, continuing the saga?

source.notes: Released November 22, 2019.

questions.q: Frozen was the first animated film to cross which box-office milestone?

source.notes: It was the highest-grossing animated film ever at the time.

questions.q: At the 2014 Oscars telecast, John Travolta famously botched Idina Menzel's name as what?

source.notes: Still reference in pop culture as the 'Adele Dazeem' moment.

questions.q: Frozen the Broadway Musical opened in which year?

source.notes: Opened March 22, 2018 at the St. James Theatre.
